    Home › Airlines › Singapore Airlines

    SQ Singapore Airlines

    Singapore · Star Alliance · Hub: Singapore Changi (SIN)

    The A380's launch operator — first airline in the world to fly the superjumbo, in October 2007.

    Types in fleet3
    Biggest aircraftAirbus A380
    AllianceStar Alliance
    Founded1972
    IATASQ
    Side by side

    The fleet at true scale

    Every type Singapore Airlines flies, drawn to the same scale — widest wings on the left. Tap any silhouette.

    Airbus A380
    Boeing 777
    Airbus A350
    The fleet

    What Singapore Airlines flies

    Grid
    12 in fleet

    Airbus A380

    Airbus · Out of production (2021) — in service

    The launch operator — first A380 flight in the world, Singapore–Sydney, October 2007.

    Full specification →
    ≈23 in fleet

    Boeing 777

    Boeing · In production (777X in certification)

    777-300ERs bridging to the 777-9 order.

    Full specification →
    65 in fleet

    Airbus A350

    Airbus · In production

    Largest A350 operator in the world — sole operator of the ULR flying the longest routes on Earth.

    Full specification →
    On order

    Boeing 777X

    Boeing · In certification — deliveries expected 2027

    31 777-9s on order.

    Full specification →
    Flies Airbus A380 — the largest passenger aircraft ever built →
    Route records

    Their longest flights

    RouteAircraftDistanceBlock time
    Singapore (SIN) → New York JFK (JFK)Airbus A350-900ULR15,332 km18 h 45
    Singapore (SIN) → Newark (EWR)Airbus A350-900ULR15,329 km18 h 30
    Singapore (SIN) → Los Angeles (LAX)Airbus A350-900ULR14,114 km17 h 50
    San Francisco (SFO) → Singapore (SIN)Airbus A350-90013,593 km17 h 25

    From our longest flights in the world board.

    Good to know

    Singapore Airlines, in a few facts

    • Operates the longest scheduled flight in the world: Singapore–New York JFK, over 15,000 km nonstop.
    • Was the global launch operator of the Airbus A380 in 2007.
    • Wi-Fi is free in every cabin class, fleet-wide.
    Sources

    • Singapore Airlines — our fleet ↗

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
